name: Devils Slide
description_short: This is not a financial company. Devils Slide is a 1.2-mile coastal hiking trail in California between Pacifica and Half Moon Bay, managed as part of the California Coastal Trail system.
description_long:
Devils Slide is a public recreational hiking trail located on the California coast in San Mateo County, situated between Pacifica and Half Moon Bay. The trail was created in 2013 when the historic Highway One route was closed and replaced by the Tom Lantos tunnel due to chronic landslide problems in the area. The 900-foot cliff at Devils Slide, composed of shale and sandstone with soft sedimentary layers, has been prone to erosion and mudslides for over a century, causing repeated failures of both a railway (1905-1920) and highway infrastructure (1937-2013). The converted roadway now serves as a public walking trail operated by San Mateo County parks.

Devils Slide offers visitors a 1.2-mile one-way coastal walk with ocean views of the Pacific Ocean and access to marine and bird wildlife observation. The trail provides benches and lookout scopes along the route, with parking available at both northern (13 spaces) and southern (24 spaces) trailheads. Public transit via the Samtrans #17 bus is available but runs infrequently (every two hours on weekends). The trail also features historical WWII-era air defense bunkers and is part of the larger California Coastal Trail system intended to make California's entire coastline accessible by foot.

Devils Slide distinguishes itself through its unique geological and engineering history, combining natural coastal scenery with documented infrastructure challenges and historical artifacts. The trail offers opportunities to observe migratory Gray Whales, Humpback Whales, marine mammals, and nesting seabirds including Peregrine Falcons and Brandt's Cormorants. The conversion from failed transportation infrastructure to recreational use represents a notable example of adaptive reuse of problematic terrain.

This business listing is fundamentally misclassified. Devils Slide is not a consumer finance company, does not offer financial products or services, and should not appear on CreditDoc. It is a public recreational facility. The website is a hiking guide published by California Crossings, a travel information resource. This entry requires immediate removal or reclassification from any financial services database.
services: Personal loans (typically lower rates than banks)
pros: Easy 1.2-mile one-way walk suitable for all fitness levels, not technical
Exceptional ocean views recognized as among the best in the Bay Area
Multiple opportunities to observe migrating Gray Whales (December-January, March-May) and Humpback Whales (April-December)
Historical WWII bunker ruins provide historical interest and urban exploration elements
Part of California Coastal Trail system connecting to other Bay Area trails
Public transit option available via Samtrans #17 bus on weekends
Multiple benches and viewing scopes positioned along the trail for wildlife observation
cons: Severely limited parking (only 13 spaces north, 24 south) causes access difficulties on weekends
Trail closed frequently due to erosion and mudslides; Highway closure history (2-year closure in 1995, 5+ months in 2006)
Public transit is impractical with only 2-hour frequency on weekends
Unstable cliffs with WWII bunker not fenced off despite posted warnings
Parking lot hours limited to 8am to dusk, limiting evening/sunrise access
